raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a second complaint against Citibank because they refused to refund {$3300.00} in unauthorized charges from a merchant called XXXX XXXX XXXX, which were billed to my Citi credit card between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/year>. I previously filed a CFPB complaint ( Complaint # XXXX ), but I was not able to submit a rebuttal to Citis response due to the limited response window. 

To clarify : I authorized a few transactions with this merchant. The charges in question were made without my consent or knowledge. I did not agree to any recurring billing, subscription, or purchases made without my consent. These transactions were unauthorized, and I noticed them only after reviewing my statements. 

Citibank responded that because I had previously done business with the merchant and provided my card information, all the charges thereafter were valid. I strongly disagree with this position. Providing my card for a few transactions does not mean I authorized {$3300.00} in charges that were made without my consent. Citi did not perform a proper investigation or request proof of my authorization for each transaction. Citibank 's XXXX attempted to call me one hour before their closing time, of which I was unable to respond due to my obligations that I had during the time. Then, they " completed '' their investigation the very next morning, without giving me adequate time to respond. 

This handling violates my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), which protects consumers from billing errors and unauthorized charges. Citi failed to meet its obligation to investigate my dispute in good faith. 

I am also filing a complaint with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency ( OCC ), as Citibank is a nationally chartered bank and I believe this is a broader issue of unfair dispute resolution practices.
